title: How Trump Decided to Go to War With Iran
summary: President Trump’s embrace of military action in Iran was spurred by an Israeli leader determined to end diplomatic negotiations. Few of the president’s advisers voiced opposition.

title: As Maduro and Khamenei Learned, It’s Harder Than Ever for Leaders to Hide
summary: A surge in sensors and cameras, combined with artificial intelligence, has transformed U.S. intelligence’s ability to locate foreign heads of state. Add to that an American president willing to capture or kill them.

title: U.S. Military Death Toll in Iran War Rises to 6
summary: The number of U.S. service members killed in the first three days of the war grew as officials said the remains of two more troops had been recovered.

title: In Republican Win, Supreme Court Retains G.O.P. District in New York
summary: A Republican congresswoman had asked the justices to preserve her New York City district, despite a lower-court ruling that it illegally diluted the power of minority voters.

title: They’re Hiring at U.S.A.I.D. Just Not Anyone Who Worked There.
summary: The Trump administration said in a memo it wanted to “avoid the risk of impaired objectivity” by hiring former staff members to wind down operations at the U.S. Agency for International Development.

title: What Is Trump’s Neck Rash? His Doctor Declines to Explain.
summary: President Trump’s physician did not explain what the skin condition was or what medication he was using to treat it.

title: Scientists Decry ‘Political Attack’ on the Reference Manual on Scientific Evidence
summary: More than two dozen contributors to the manual criticized the deletion of a chapter on climate science by the Federal Judicial Center.

title: What the Pentagon’s Attack Videos Reveal About the Boat Strikes at Sea
summary: The military has released more than 40 video clips of U.S. attacks on vessels it says are trafficking drugs. But they tell only part of the story.

title: As Kennedy Turns From Vaccines, MAHA Allies See a ‘Betrayal’
summary: As the midterm elections approach, the health secretary is focusing on “real food.” Anger is rising among some of his supporters.

title: N.Y. Attorney General Orders Hospital to Resume Youth Transgender Care
summary: NYU Langone Health had stopped providing puberty-blocking medication and hormone treatments after the federal government threatened to pull its funding.

title: Meeting on U.S. Measles Status Is Delayed Until November
summary: The decision to revoke the country’s elimination status would now likely come after the U.S. midterm elections, experts noted.

title: States Move to Limit Access to H.I.V. Treatment
summary: Citing rising costs and shortfalls in federal support, about 20 states are toughening eligibility requirements for patients in drug assistance programs.

title: America’s Billionaires Continue to Flock to Wyoming
summary: Jackson, Wyo., has long been a refuge for the rich. But the last five years saw a boom in wealth of a kind never before seen. Across the country, the 2017 tax cuts minted hundreds of new billionaires.

title: The Pentagon’s Favorite Tech Guy Is This Hawaiian Shirt-Wearing Founder
summary: Palmer Luckey, who founded the defense tech start-up Anduril, has become the It Guy as President Trump aims to modernize the U.S. military with autonomous weapons.

title: Investigators Examine Iran War as Possible Motive in Austin Shooting
summary: The shooting happened about 26 hours after the United States and Israel attacked Iran. Officials identified the two who died in the shooting and said a third victim would be taken off life support.

title: Texas Tech Student Identified as One of the Victims in Austin Shooting
summary: Ryder Harrington, 19, and Savitha Shan, 21, were confirmed dead by officials on Monday. The shooting is being investigated as a possible act of terrorism.

title: The Night, and the Gun, That Changed Two Lives
summary: An encounter between a troubled man and a crime-fearing man left one of them dead.

title: Macron Expands French Nuclear Arsenal and Vows Protection for Neighbors
summary: The announcement was a significant step in forging a European deterrent as Russia grows more aggressive and the U.S. steps back.

title: Prosecutor Says Father ‘Could Have Prevented’ Georgia School Shooting
summary: Before closing arguments in the trial, the father, Colin Gray, testified that he never considered his son capable of the attack that killed four people in 2024.

title: When ‘Anora’ Becomes ‘Anorak’: The Art of Wrong Movie Titles
summary: Staffers at Film at Lincoln Center keep a list of the incorrect movie titles they’ve heard from patrons. That list is very, very long.

title: Trump Was Never Antiwar
summary: Trump’s foreign policy has often been less a repudiation of neoconservatism than a mutation of it.

title: Americans Need to Know More About The Iran Attacks
summary: The American public has received too little information to effectively judge the goals and objectives of the largest U.S. military operation in the Middle East in a generation.

title: Trump Foresees an Extended War
summary: Also, how the U.S. decided to strike Iran. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.

title: U.S. Slaps Sanctions on Rwanda, Saying It Sabotaged Trump Peace Deal
summary: Rwanda’s government responded by claiming the sanctions unjustly targeted only one party to the conflict and misrepresented the facts.

title: Your Closest Forest Gets Lit Up by a Hidden Purple Glow
summary: Research shows that a phenomenon called St. Elmo’s Fire, which occurs during thunderstorms, may be much more common than previously understood.

title: Blood Moon Lunar Eclipse 2026: Time and Visibility Guide
summary: Earth’s shadow will temporarily drape the moon in a copper hue before sunrise on Tuesday.

title: Half of Colorectal Cancer Cases Now Occur in Those Under 65, Study Shows
summary: It isn’t clear what’s driving the shift, but scientists suspect that environmental exposures may be partially to blame.
